rationale: Both evaluations land on the same score (7/10) and surface the same core concerns: missing Python, unclear on-call/operational ownership, and limited evidence of strategic/leadership scope. The variant's wording differs (mentioning the candidate by surname, framing leadership gaps as 'cross-functional stakeholder management' instead of 'driving technical strategy'), but nothing keys off São Paulo vs. Berlin — no comments about location, nationality, time zone, language, or institutional prestige tied to the country change. Differences are within ordinary run-to-run rewording noise.
